title: Why is only one seed of life allowed on a planet
display_title: Why is only one seed of life allowed on a planet
wikitext:
[]: Why is only one seed of life allowed on a planet?             Quote Post by Gendos Glasses » Wed Jul 24, 2024 1:52 am So I thought about why the FAR made the hard rule about only one seed of life being allowed per planet. They wanted this rule enforced so hard, they even sent a spear of Longinus with each moon to cover the edge case. It just makes no sense to me, is it revealed somewhere?

[]: Re: Why is only one seed of life allowed on a planet?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Jul 24, 2024 10:33 am I guess it's the rule of nature. We know Adam’s children and Lilins can't coexist. One was given the Fruit of Life, the other the Fruit of Knowledge. They are incompatible. The whole FAR backstory isn't in the original series, but elements of it are suggested in there. I think each Angel (Lilith, Adam and maybe others we don't know about) were made to carry one specific Fruit and this was maybe evidenced by the color of their moon (Adam's is white, Lilith's is black). And because the Fruits are incompatible, they can't coexist in the same planet.

[]: Re: Why is only one seed of life allowed on a planet?             Quote Post by Gendos Glasses » Wed Jul 24, 2024 10:43 am That they can't coexist is a good point but it raises the next question. If two land on a planet, the spear of Longinus deactivates one which results in one species not living. If the spear of Longinus wouldn't exist, the both species would fight each other also resulting in one species not living, so what's the point? I apologise for my rusty english in advance.

[]: Re: Why is only one seed of life allowed on a planet?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Aug 12, 2024 5:19 am My headcanon was always that the FAR civilization destroyed itself somehow (rather like the precursors from Nadia), and that's why they were seeding other worlds in the first place. They didn't want their "children" to be as powerful as the FAR themselves so that they wouldn't be able to wreak the same amount of havoc. I imagine that the closest we've seen to what the FAR may have been like is Kaworu.

[]: Re: Why is only one seed of life allowed on a planet?             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon Aug 12, 2024 9:42 am Gendos Glasses wrote: That they can't coexist is a good point but it raises the next question. If two land on a planet, the spear of Longinus deactivates one which results in one species not living. If the spear of Longinus wouldn't exist, the both species would fight each other also resulting in one species not living, so what's the point? I apologise for my rusty english in advance.
